s59950832.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  There is no focal consolidation or pneumothorax.  There is
increased prominence and cephalization of the pulmonary vascular markings
consistent with pulmonary edema.  The heart is enlarged but stable compared to
prior study.  Prominence of the left hilum likely represents enlarged
pulmonary artery and thus pulmonary hypertension. There is calcification of
the aortic knob. There is stable blunting of the left costophrenic angle
likely due to chronic pleural thickening.  There are also pleural plaques seen
indicative of asbestos-related pleural disease.  Incidental note is made of an
azygos fissure.  A two-lead right-sided pacemaker in stable position without
complication.
s59950832.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:
1. Pulmonary edema with stable cardiomegaly.
2. Prominent pulmonary artery likely related to pulmonary hypertension
3. Stable asbestos-related pleural disease and blunting of the left
costophrenic angle likely due to pleural thickening.

s52147659.txt.impression:

s50175211.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:  AP chest compared to ___:

Tracheostomy tube in standard placement.  No mediastinal widening or
pneumothorax.  Severe peribronchiolar infiltration, left mid and lower lung
and the right lower lung zones has worsened since ___, attributable to
more severe aspiration.  Since heart size remains normal and there is no
appreciable vascular congestion, I cannot attribute this to pulmonary edema
superimposed on changes of chronic aspiration.  Pleural effusions are small if
any.  No pneumothorax.  Right supraclavicular central venous line ends in the
right atrium, left PIC line in the low SVC as before.  Aortic endograft
unchanged in position, aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta.

s55828111.txt.findings:

The patient is status post right upper lobectomy with volume loss in the right

s59081261.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

Moderate to severe cardiomegaly is again noted.  The aortic knob is calcified.

Hazy opacity within the left lower lobe likely reflects atelectasis.  There is

detected.
s59081261.txt.impression:

Mildly elevated central venous pressures in the setting of moderate to severe

s53921371.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Compared to chest radiographs ___ through ___ at 04:34.

Lung volumes have improved.  Asymmetric opacification of the hemi thoraces
continues.  Some of the abnormality on the right is due to persistent pleural
effusion, but I suspect most of it is due to pneumonia, perhaps aspiration.
There is also a moderate left pleural effusion.  No pneumothorax.  Heart size
normal.

Severe upper lobe scarring is long-standing.

s50824082.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

1.  Right subclavian Port-A-Cath has its tip in the distal SVC, unchanged.
Although lung volumes are relatively low, the overall interstitium appears
within normal limits.  No focal airspace consolidation is appreciated at this
time.  Overall, cardiac and mediastinal contours are stable.  No pneumothorax
is seen.  Please note, however, that chest CT is far more sensitive to detect
ground-glass opacity than a conventional plain film.

s50861865.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  PA and lateral chest views were obtained with patient in upright
position.  Analysis is performed in direct comparison with the next preceding
similar study of ___.  Marked cardiomegaly appears stable.
Moderate enlargement of the left atrium and the pulmonary vasculature
demonstrates an upper zone redistribution pattern.  Some haze exists in the
basal vascularity with some interstitial increased structures, all consistent
with mild degree of chronic left-sided CHF.  Thoracic aorta has not changed in
size and configuration and calcium deposits exist in the wall at the level of
the arch as before.  There presently no signs of acute discrete pulmonary
parenchymal infiltrates and the on previous examination identified scattered
patchy parenchymal densities have disappeared.
s50861865.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:  Stable significant cardiac enlargement with evidence of chronic
pulmonary congestion but no evidence of acute edema or pleural effusions.  The
on previous examinations identified suspected pneumonic infiltrates on the
right lung base have disappeared.

s50461458.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

The cardiac, mediastinal and hilar contours appear unchanged.  There is no

s50461458.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Moderate interstitial abnormality suggesting pulmonary edema in addition to

s50935325.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

In comparison with the chest radiographs obtained ___, no significant
changes are appreciated.  Extensive, bilateral, nodular opacities are grossly
unchanged and compatible with extensive, known metastatic disease. Small,
right pleural effusion is unchanged.  Extensive sclerosis of the vertebral
bodies compatible with bony metastasis is also grossly unchanged without
evidence of fracture.  No obvious consolidations compatible with pneumonia.
Heart size is normal without pulmonary edema.  Elevation of the left
hemidiaphragm and gaseous distention of the adjacent colon are unchanged.
Port-A-Cath tip projects over the right atrium.
s50935325.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Extensive intrathoracic metastatic disease as described above is grossly
unchanged.  No superimposed acute cardiopulmonary abnormalities.

s51028497.txt.findings:

appear within normal limits.  The chest is hyperinflated.  There are no

s51028497.txt.impression:

s58967573.txt.impression:

s58389209.txt.impression:

No acute cardiopulmonary process.  COPD.

s52657162.txt.findings:

s59108512.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Left lower lobe is still densely consolidated and there is a stable moderately
severe left pleural abnormality. Right lung is grossly clear.  There may be
new small right pleural effusion.  Moderate cardiomegaly unchanged.  No
pneumothorax.  ET tube in standard placement.  Nasogastric tube passes into

s51268796.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

As compared to the previous radiograph, the lung volumes have increased,
likely reflecting improved ventilation.  The extent of the pre-existing areas
of atelectasis at both the left and the right lung base has decreased. Also
decreased are the small bilateral pleural effusions. Moderate cardiomegaly
remains. No pulmonary edema. A zone of parenchymal opacity surrounding the the
left hilus is unchanged.  Normal alignment of the sternal wires. No
pneumothorax.

s58108137.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

The lungs are well-expanded and clear.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
Heart size, mediastinal contour, and hila are unremarkable.

s55611698.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Right-sided pacer remains in place with leads terminating over the expected
location of the right atrium and right ventricle, respectively.  There is
worsening bilateral airspace and perihilar vascular congestion, consistent
with mild-to-moderate pulmonary edema.  There are increasing bilateral
layering effusions.  Bibasilar airspace opacities likely reflect compressive
atelectasis.  Overall, cardiac and mediastinal contours are likely unchanged
given differences in positioning.  Several of the superior sternal wires are
fractured, but unchanged.  No pneumothorax is seen.  Severe degenerative
changes are seen of an incompletely visualized left glenohumeral joint, where
there is also flattening of the humeral head which may represent a component
of avascular necrosis.  Clinical correlation is advised.

s58534705.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  Interval extubation.  Apparent interval removal of nasogastric
tube, although a tube is coiled overlying the lower portion of the neck,
incompletely imaged on this study.  Clinical correlation suggested to exclude
coiling of NG tube in the cervical esophagus.  Stable cardiomegaly allowing
for lower lung volumes.  Resolution of linear right juxtahilar atelectasis but
development of linear atelectasis at the left lung base.  No pleural effusion
or pneumothorax.

s57139642.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

Moderate enlargement of the cardiac silhouette is re- demonstrated.  The aorta
remains tortuous.  Mediastinal and hilar contours are unchanged.  Pulmonary
vasculature is normal.  There is likely minimal atelectasis within both lung
bases.  No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen.
There are no acute osseous abnormalities.
s57139642.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Unchanged moderate cardiomegaly.  Mild bibasilar atelectasis.

s56707296.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:  AP chest compared to ___:

Widespread severe and somewhat heterogeneous pulmonary consolidation
progressed from ___ through ___.  Subsequently, the patient
has been extubated, increasing lung volumes and generally responsible for
improvement in the appearance of the lungs.  Since there has been no
subsequent improvement, I presume the process is at least as severe as it was
earlier.  It is so extensive that I cannot distinguish somewhat asymmetric
edema alone from some edema and multifocal pneumonia, which is most likely.
Heart is only mildly enlarged.  Pleural effusions are presumed, but not
substantial.  No pneumothorax.  ET tube is in standard position.  Right
jugular line ends at the origin of the SVC.  An upper enteric drainage tube
passes into the stomach and out of view.  There is no pneumothorax.

s50139755.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

Cardiac, mediastinal and hilar contours are normal.  Pulmonary vasculature is
normal.  Again demonstrated within the left lower lobe is a triangular area of
opacification which appears improved compared to the prior exam, likely the
residua of prior necrotizing pneumonia.  Minimal blunting of the left
costophrenic sulcus may suggest chronic pleural thickening or fluid.  No new
areas of focal consolidation otherwise demonstrated.  There is no
pneumothorax.  No acute osseous abnormality seen.
s50139755.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Triangular area of opacification in the left lower lobe likely reflects
sequela of prior necrotizing pneumonia, and is improved compared to the prior
radiograph.  Blunting of the right costophrenic angle may be due to chronic
pleural thickening or trace fluid.  No new areas of focal consolidation are
demonstrated to suggest pneumonia.

s50812606.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  There is a vague opacity in the right mid lung zone.  The left lung
is clear.  There is persistent blunting of the right costophrenic angle,
likely due to a small persistent right pleural effusion.  It comparison to the
prior exam, it has slightly decreased in size.  There is no left pleural
effusion.  There is no pneumothorax.  The cardiomediastinal silhouette is
normal.
s50812606.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:
1.  Vague right mid lung opacity which is of uncertain etiology, although
could represent an early pneumonia.  Recommend further evaluation with oblique
views.
2.  Interval decrease in size of the right pleural effusion.

Results were discussed with Dr. ___ ___ resident) at 7:50 AM on ___
via telephone by Dr. ___ ___ the findings were discovered upon attending
review.

s57005451.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  PA and lateral views of the chest were obtained.  Lung volumes are
low which somewhat limits evaluation.  Fluid is noted tracking along the
fissural surfaces.  Mild interstitial pulmonary edema is noted.  There is no
focal consolidation to suggest pneumonia.  No pneumothorax.  Cardiomediastinal
silhouette is stable with an unfolded thoracic aorta.  Bony structures are
stable with multiple lower thoracic and upper lumbar compression fractures
better assessed on a prior CT torso from ___.  IVC filter is partially
visualized in the upper abdomen on the lateral view as well as surgical clips
in the upper quadrant.
